task: Fix Discord webhook error reporting by exposing HTTP errors in send_message return value
discussion_summary: User reported that Discord notifications were failing silently with only a generic warning message. The root cause was that send_message() was catching exceptions and returning False without exposing the actual HTTP error. Session refactored send_message() to return a tuple (success: bool, error_message: str) and updated all callers in askr.py and notification.py to unpack and handle the error details. This enables proper debugging of webhook configuration issues.

accomplishments.what: Modified discord.py send_message() to return tuple (bool, str) with actual HTTP error details instead of swallowing exceptions
Updated askr.py callers of send_message() to unpack tuple and log error messages
Updated notification.py hook to unpack send_message() tuple return value
Verified all other callers of send_message() are safe with tuple return (Python doesn't enforce bool type checking)

in_progress.what: Return tuple (success, error_message) from send_message() to expose HTTP errors
Unpack send_message() tuple return and log error details in init and session handlers
Unpack send_message() tuple return in notification hook

next_actions.action: Complete the git commit that was started (git add ... && git commit -m 'fix: expose Discord webhook HTTP errors in send_message return tuple')
next_actions.why: Changes are staged but commit was interrupted; need to finalize before testing

next_actions.action: Run askr init again and verify the Discord webhook error message now shows the actual HTTP error (e.g., 401 Unauthorized, 404 Not Found, etc.) instead of generic warning
next_actions.why: Confirms that error details are now visible for debugging webhook configuration issues

next_actions.action: Test with invalid ASKR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK value to verify error message is informative
next_actions.why: Validates that the tuple unpacking and error logging works end-to-end

next_actions.action: Check if ASKR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK is actually set in ~/.config/askr/.env or if it's empty/missing
next_actions.why: User's error suggests webhook may not be configured at all; need to verify setup_keys() is saving it correctly

decisions.decision: Return tuple (bool, str) from send_message() instead of just bool
decisions.reason: Allows callers to access actual HTTP error details for debugging; swallowing exceptions was preventing diagnosis of webhook issues

decisions.decision: Keep all callers that ignore return value unchanged (Python doesn't enforce type checking)
decisions.reason: Simpler than refactoring every caller; only the two that check the return value needed updates

failed_approaches.approach: Checking if send_message() return value was being used as a bool in all callers before making changes
failed_approaches.reason: Unnecessary caution; Python allows tuple to be used in boolean context without error, so all existing callers remain safe

relational_files.why: Manages ASKR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK configuration; relevant to understanding why webhook might be missing/invalid
Uses send_message() in notification hook; was checked to ensure tuple return doesn't break it

blockers: Git commit was started but not completed (git add ... && g was cut off); need to finish commit before next session can verify changes
